revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:穴水大宮]]


{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]
|name = Anamizu Ōmiya
|location = Ōmachi Ho-1, Anamizu Town, Hōsu District, Ishikawa Prefecture
| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]
|deity =  {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjoshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}
|image = File:Anamizu Ohmiya Jinja.jpg
|coordinates = {{Coord|37.227556|N|136.756583|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
|Deities = {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime no Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, {{ill|Shoichii Inari Daimyōjin|ja|正一位稲荷大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoichii Inari Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, others
|Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor), {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}{{efn|District Shrines|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}
|location = {{ill|Anamizu|qid=Q1349353|1=Anamizu, Ishikawa}}
}}
'''Anamizu Ōmiya''' (穴水大宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Anamizu, Ishikawa|en|Anamizu, Ishikawa|qid=Q1349353|lt=Anamizu, Ishikawa|1=Anamizu, Ishikawa}}, Ishikawa Prefecture. It is identified with the {{ill|Hetsuhime Shrine|ja|辺津比咩神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hetsuhime Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135503322|1=Hetsuhime Shrine}} mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|qid=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}}, and its former Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qid=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}{{efn|District Shrines|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}.
== Overview ==
Known as Hetsuhime Shrine (辺津比咩神社) in the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|qid=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}}, the shrine is widely revered under the name Anamizu Ōmiya. It is renowned for drawing significant crowds for New Year's and {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|en|Shichi-Go-San|qid=Q1072387|lt=Shichi-Go-San|1=Shichi-Go-San}} visits, Ranking among the most popular shrines in the Noto region.
Beneath the main sanctuary lies a mystical stone chamber measuring roughly six shaku by six shaku. It is said to contain ancient treasures and documents from the divine era. The name "Anamizu" (穴水) is derived from the sacred spring "Manai" flowing from the cave called Sakuratani.
During the period from the Genroku to Kyōhō eras, the shrine was under the patronage of the Tokugawa family, with a magistrate stationed locally. Donations such as golden lanterns, which still exist today, reflect its esteemed status. The shrine is also home to the oldest {{ill|Ema (Shinto)|en|Ema (Shinto)|qid=Q687288|lt=Ema (Shinto)|1=Ema (Shinto)}} (votive picture tablet) in Hokuriku, depicting a sailboat. Additionally, a waka poem dedicated by {{ill|Saigyō|en|Saigyō|qid=Q224689|lt=Saigyō|1=Saigyō}} during his visit in the Eiji era is among its treasures.
In ancient times, gentle waves would lap at the shrine’s front, forming a bay known as Hetsu no Irie, where boats could be moored. The title "Shoichii" (正一位) signifies the highest Rank in divine honors.
The shrine suffered significant damage during the {{ill|2024 Noto earthquake|lt=2024 Noto Peninsula Earthquake|ja|能登半島地震 (2024th year )|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2024 Noto earthquake|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on January 1, 2024. The four-legged torii gate collapsed, and the hand-washing pavilion was destroyed.<ref>{{Cite web |title=石川・穴水の神社も被災　鳥居や手水舎崩れ落ちる　能登半島地震 |url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20240102/k00/00m/040/249000c |website=毎日新聞 |date=2024-01-02 |access-date=2024-01-21 |language=ja|qq=}}</ref>
== Deities ==
The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjoshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}:
* {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}}
* {{ill|Tagitsuhime no Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}}
* {{ill|Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}
Additional deities include:
* {{ill|inari|lt=Shoichii Inari Daimyōjin|ja|正一位稲荷大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/inari|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Ōyamakui|ja|大山咋命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamakui|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大山咋神|1=Oyamakui no Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10935105}}
* {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi no Mikoto|ja|加具頭知命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kagutsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|ukanomitama|qid=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama no Mikoto|ja|宇迦魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|1=Ukanomitama}}
* {{ill|sukunabikona|lt=Sukunabikona no Mikoto|ja|少名彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}
== Festivals ==
* '''April 28–29''': Gōsha Festival
* '''June 30''': Ōharai Ceremony
* '''September''': Hōtō Festival
== References ==
{{Reflist|qq=}}
== External Links ==
